client_context.facility_line: Munich Bread Works & Vienna Pastries, Germany / Austria
client_context.jurisdiction_label: EU, German & Austrian Sustainability Frameworks for Bakery Manufacturers

executive_summary:
November 2025 opens Brösel Backwaren GmbH's regulatory screening cycle with a dense cluster of packaging and sustainability obligations arising from the company's dual German and Austrian operating footprint. The EU PPWR framework (EU-PPWR-2025/40) is the dominant theme, with the Commission's consultation on recyclability criteria directly targeting the multilayer barrier films used in Brösel's premium artisan bread packaging at Munich Bread Works. The company uses a combination of modified-atmosphere PE/EVOH laminate bags for extended-shelf-life sliced bread (approximately 680 tonnes/year) and single-use PP bakery bags for pastry lines at Vienna Pastries.

The DE-VerpackDG-2026 draft amendment to Germany's Verpackungsdurchführungsgesetz has been published for consultation and introduces Article 12c fee differentiation penalising packaging with non-recyclable barrier films. Brösel's Munich sliced bread PE/EVOH laminates are likely in the high-fee tier. The Bundesrat summer recess vote is scheduled for May 2026, giving Brösel a six-month window to model the financial impact and engage through the Zentralverband des Deutschen Bäckerhandwerks.

The AT-EAG-2021 Austrian Extended Producer Responsibility (Elektro- und Elektronikgeräte-Altgeräte-Verordnung) scheme, effective January 2025, extends EPR obligations to food-sector packaging producers above a 15-tonne annual threshold. Vienna Pastries places approximately 78 tonnes of packaging on the Austrian market annually, confirming in-scope status. Brösel must register with Austria's Altstoff Recycling Austria (ARA) scheme and pay annual EPR contributions based on material category and weight.

sections.heading: EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation
paragraphs: PPWR establishes binding recyclability criteria for all EU-market packaging. Brösel's Munich Bread Works PE/EVOH laminate bags for modified-atmosphere sliced bread packaging are at risk of Class C classification under the draft delegated act, given the EVOH barrier layer. Vienna Pastries' single-use PP bakery bags are likely Class B (recyclable with process improvements). Commission's November 2025 call for evidence on the recyclability criteria for bakery and confectionery packaging specifically references multilayer MA barrier films as a priority assessment category.

Regulation (EU) 2025/40 (EUR-Lex 32025R0040). For bakery sector manufacturers, the regulation's key provisions include: (1) recyclability by design requirements applying from 2028, using a 10-category scoring system to be defined by delegated act; (2) minimum recycled content requirements from 2030 (10% for food-contact packaging); (3) extended producer responsibility fee modulation based on recyclability tier; and (4) single-use plastic restrictions that affect PP bakery bags if recyclability cannot be demonstrated. The Commission's sector-specific annex in the November 2025 consultation document identifies 'bakery and confectionery multilayer barrier packaging' as a category requiring specific assessment methodology due to the food safety requirements that drive the use of barrier layers.
sections.callout: Register for Commission's January 2026 public consultation on recyclability criteria through Zentralverband des Deutschen Bäckerhandwerks; commission packaging LCA for Munich Bread Works bread packaging formats.

sections.heading: Verpackungsdurchführungsgesetz Amendment
paragraphs: Germany's draft VerpackDG-2026 amendment introduces differentiated EPR fee structures penalising non-recyclable packaging. Brösel's Munich Bread Works PE/EVOH laminates (680 tonnes/year) are likely in the high-fee tier (EUR 0.05–0.18/kg). The amendment also requires all packaging producers to report packaging material compositions to the Zentrale Stelle Verpackungsregister (ZSVR) using a standardised digital declaration format (PackML) by 30 June 2026.

The draft VerpackDG-2026 amendment was published for parliamentary consultation by the Bundesumweltministerium in October 2025, with consultation period running to 31 March 2026. The amendment transposes and extends the PPWR fee modulation requirements into German EPR law, creating a tiered surcharge on top of the existing ZSVR system fees. Article 12c introduces fee multipliers of 1.0x (Class A), 1.4x (Class B), 2.0x (Class C) of the base material-specific EPR fee. For PE/EVOH laminate at the Class C assessment, Brösel's estimated current EPR contribution of EUR 52,000/year for Munich Bread Works packaging could increase to EUR 104,000/year. The ZSVR PackML digital declaration requirement creates a new data management obligation for all German-market packaging producers.
sections.callout: Prepare PackML digital packaging composition declaration for Munich Bread Works by 30 June 2026; model Article 12c fee exposure for PE/EVOH laminate formats.

sections.heading: Austrian Extended Producer Responsibility — Food Packaging (ARA Scheme)
paragraphs: Austria's revised Verpackungsverordnung 2025 (implementing AT-EAG-2021 for food packaging) requires all operators placing more than 15 tonnes of packaging on the Austrian market annually to register with the Altstoff Recycling Austria (ARA) scheme and pay EPR contributions. Vienna Pastries places approximately 78 tonnes/year, confirming in-scope status. Annual ARA contribution is estimated at EUR 18,200 based on current published rates for paper/board (EUR 0.15/kg) and plastic (EUR 0.28/kg) materials.

Austria's Verpackungsverordnung 2025 (BGBl. II Nr. 4/2025) updated the EPR framework for food packaging producers, aligning with the forthcoming PPWR requirements and introducing material-disaggregated contribution rates. The ARA scheme (altstoffrecyclingaustria.at) provides online registration and accepts digital packaging quantity declarations via the ARA-Online-Service portal. Non-registration triggers an administrative penalty of EUR 5,000–50,000 under the Abfallwirtschaftsgesetz 2002. Vienna Pastries' packaging mix consists of approximately 51 tonnes of paper/board (pastry boxes, bags) and 27 tonnes of plastic (PP bakery bags, cellulose overwrap), generating estimated annual ARA contributions of EUR 7,650 (paper) + EUR 7,560 (plastic) = EUR 15,210, plus administration fees.
sections.callout: Register Vienna Pastries with ARA by 31 January 2026; provide material-level packaging quantity declarations for FY2025 by 31 March 2026.

sections.heading: Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz Amendments
paragraphs: The LkSG (German Supply Chain Due Diligence Act) applies to Brösel Backwaren GmbH if the company employs more than 1,000 employees in Germany. With Munich Bread Works employing approximately 680 workers and Vienna Pastries adding another 340 in Austria, the German employee count alone puts Brösel at the threshold. Companies at the threshold must conduct an annual human rights and environmental risk assessment covering direct and indirect suppliers — including flour millers, sugar producers, and palm oil-using ingredient suppliers.

The Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz (LkSG) entered force 1 January 2023 for companies with >3,000 German employees, expanding to >1,000 from 1 January 2024. The 2023 amendments extended the Act's scope and introduced enhanced civil liability provisions aligned with the forthcoming CSDDD. BAFA enforces compliance through annual report review and has authority to impose fines of up to EUR 8 million or 2% of global annual turnover for material non-compliance. For food manufacturers, supply chain human rights risks typically include palm oil sourcing from Indonesia/Malaysia (land rights), cocoa from West Africa (child labour), and seasonal worker conditions in domestic agricultural supply chains. Brösel's flour sourcing from German mills with established union recognition is lower risk; its butter and sugar supply chains require further mapping.
sections.callout: Confirm German employee count for LkSG threshold determination; if at or above 1,000, file LkSG compliance report with BAFA (Bundesamt für Wirtschaft und Ausfuhrkontrolle) by 30 April 2026 for FY2025.

references.citation: Regulation (EU) 2025/40 on packaging and packaging waste
